target.title: fix(auth): skip no-op auth-profile disk writes on success
target.body:
## Summary

`markAuthProfileSuccess` persists `auth-profiles.json` on **every** successful model call, even when the only change is the `lastUsed` timestamp. Because `buildModelsJsonFingerprint` folds `auth-profiles.json`'s mtime into the `models.json` resolution cache key, this redundant write invalidates the model cache on every message, forcing a full model re-resolution per message for plugin-backed providers.

## Changes

**`src/agents/auth-profiles/profiles.ts`**:

- Add `hasActiveFailureState()` helper that checks whether a profile has any active error/block/cooldown/failure counts
- In the `markAuthProfileSuccess` updater callback, track whether `lastGood` changed or failure state needs clearing
- Return `lastGoodChanged || failureStateToClear` instead of unconditional `true`, so the file write is skipped when only `lastUsed` changed (the common case on steady-state calls)
- `lastUsed` is still updated in memory for any in-process reads

**`src/agents/auth-profiles/profiles.test.ts`**:

- Add test: "skips file write when lastGood is already set and no failure state exists" (verifies mtime is unchanged)
- Add test: "writes file when clearing failure state on success" (verifies disk is updated when errorCount > 0 and blockedUntil is set)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `markAuthProfileSuccess` writes `auth-profiles.json` on every successful model call even when nothing meaningful changed, invalidating the `models.json` resolution cache and causing unnecessary filesystem I/O.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS 15.5, Node v22.19, OpenClaw built from patched source at `~/openclaw` (branch `fix/auth-profile-success-skip-noop-write`).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  ```bash
  cd ~/openclaw
  pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
  pnpm build
  grep -n 'hasActiveFailureState\|lastGoodChanged\|failureStateToClear' dist/profiles-CWxa9Eih.js
  npx vitest run src/agents/auth-profiles/profiles.test.ts --reporter verbose
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:** terminal output from the patched build:

  The fix is active in the production bundle:

  ```console
  $ grep -n 'hasActiveFailureState\|lastGoodChanged\|failureStateToClear' dist/profiles-CWxa9Eih.js
  30:function hasActiveFailureState(stats) {
  185:const lastGoodChanged = freshStore.lastGood?.[providerKey] !== profileId;
  186:const failureStateToClear = hasActiveFailureState(freshStore.usageStats?.[profileId]);
  192:return lastGoodChanged || failureStateToClear;
  ```

  Terminal confirms the no-op write is correctly suppressed and failure-state clears are persisted:

  ```console
  ✓ |agents-core| markAuthProfileSuccess > skips file write when lastGood is already set and no failure state exists 129ms
  ✓ |agents-core| markAuthProfileSuccess > writes file when clearing failure state on success 86ms
  ✓ |agents-support| markAuthProfileSuccess > skips file write when lastGood is already set and no failure state exists 127ms
  ✓ |agents-support| markAuthProfileSuccess > writes file when clearing failure state on success 83ms
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
       Tests  28 passed (28)
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** The compiled code at `dist/profiles-CWxa9Eih.js:185-192` correctly checks whether `lastGood` changed or failure state needs clearing before returning true (triggering disk write). Terminal output confirms the mtime-based test verifies no file write occurs when only `lastUsed` changes, while failure state changes are properly persisted to disk.
- **What was not tested:** Live multi-provider session with real API credentials to measure the latency improvement end-to-end.

Closes #88654
target.author: sebtardif
